Off Calendar Feb-11-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jack Butler, Esq. 415-281-4162 of Peterson Martin Reynolds for Plaintiff.
Richard Hylen, 415-974-5580 Defendant in Pro Per.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares its views. Defendant argues amount claimed.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF MARGARET RYDER's Motion To Add Satel Group, Inc. As Judgment Debtor.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.Plaintiff Ryder's motion to add Satel Group, Inc. ("Group") as a judgment debtor per Code of Civil Procedure, 187 is granted. Satel, Inc. and Group have not opposed the motion and Hylen's opposition fails to address plaintiff's arguments. The court further notes that Hylen cannot appear for the corporations because he is not an attorney. (See Merco Constr. Engineers, Inc. v. Municipal Crt. (1978) 21 Cal.3d 724, 727.) The court previously gave Hylen an opportunity to obtain counsel for the entities but he failed to do so.

"[W]hen a corporation sells or transfers all of its assets to another corporation constituting its '"'mere continuation,'"' the latter is also liable for the former's debts and liabilities." (Wolf Metals Inc. v. Rand Pacific Sales, Inc. (2016) 4 Cal.App.5th 698, 704-705.) A successor corporation is deemed a mere continuation of the predecessor corporation "upon a showing of one or both of the following factual elements: (1) no adequate consideration was given for the predecessor corporation's assets and made available for meeting the claims of its unsecured creditors; (2) one or more persons were officers, directors, or stockholders of both corporations." (Ray v. Alad Corp. (1977) 19 Cal.3d 22, 29.) "Thus, where the successor corporation is a mere continuation and hence liable for the acts of its predecessor, the liability of the new corporation may be enforced?[by] a motion pursuant to the procedural mechanism of section 187?in order to add an additional judgment debtor." (McClellan v. Northridge Park Townhome Owners Ass'n, Inc. (2001) 89 Cal.App.4th 746, 754.)

Here, the undisputed evidence demonstrates that Group is a mere continuation of Satel, Inc. and it is liable for Satel, Inc.'s debts. Hylen was the sole shareholder and officer of Satel, Inc. (Hylen Order of Examination, 5:24-6:11; 20:2-15.) Hylen controls Group and there are no other officers. (Id. at 23:6-24:4.) Group acquired all of Satel, Inc.'s shares for nothing. (Id. at 8:15-22; 9:3-6.) Group does the same business as Satel, Inc. (Id. at 18:17-24.)

Order and Amended Judgment are signed. (see scanned order and amended judgment for details)
